title: Ghana farm activity defaults (diesel, electricity, yield guides)
description: Screening-level diesel (L/ha/yr), grid electricity (kWh/ha/yr), and yield guides for Ghana farms when the Equipment & Energy step has no measured fuel/electricity. Keyed by mechanization × irrigated. Values are research-bounded Tier-1 activity defaults — not model outputs. Uncertainty is wide (factor ~2); pedigree = estimated / not measured. Apply only for Ghana assessments; never invent fertiliser rates here.

provenance.span: Most Ghana crop operations remain hand-tool / animal traction; MoFA and CSAIP characterise rainfed smallholders without tractor hire. Screening default: 0 L diesel/ha and 0 kWh grid/ha when production_system is rainfed and mechanization is fully manual (not the legacy flat 80 L + 200 kWh).
provenance.bounds: diesel 0; electricity 0; yield guides MoFA/FAO national screening spans

provenance.span: Bucket/manual irrigation adds labour water lifting, not grid electricity or tractor diesel. Keep energy at 0/0; yield guide raised vs rainfed screening.

Small-scale irrigation in Ghana commonly uses diesel pumpsets rather than grid pumps. Screening mid-range ~50 L diesel/ha/yr (literature band roughly 20–100 L/ha for seasonal pumping); grid kWh left at 0 unless the farmer reports electricity.
Partial mechanization (hire plough / sheller) on rainfed land: modest diesel for land prep and haulage. Below the retired flat 80 L/ha assumption; West African hire-plough screening ~15–40 L/ha.
Partial mechanization plus pumped irrigation: land-prep diesel plus pump diesel. Prefer diesel over grid kWh for Ghana SSI screening.
Fully mechanized rainfed operations (own/hired tractor suite): screening ~60 L diesel/ha/yr within the broader 50–150 L/ha mechanized band cited historically; no default grid electricity.
Fully mechanized + irrigated: diesel for field ops plus modest grid electricity when electric pumps are plausible. Still below the retired 80 L + 200 kWh flat pair; wide uncertainty (factor 2).

sources.note: Tier 1 activity-data approach and wet-tropical EF1 already used in-engine; this table supplies missing activity amounts, not emission factors.
CSAIP / MoFA smallholder characterisation: rainfed, largely manual operations dominate; irrigation and mechanization are the exception.
National screening yield guides (replace a single 1500 kg/ha maize figure with crop-group spans). Not used to overwrite farmer-reported quantity_kg.
West African smallholder mechanization and SSI pump literature: fully manual ≈ 0 diesel; hire plough modest L/ha; SSI often diesel-pumped rather than grid.
